title: Comforting Dense Ukrainian Haggis Casserole
description: A winey, dense, hangover-cure Ukrainian dish featuring haggis, miso-marinate, and paleo-friendly ingredients, served in a ramekin. This hearty casserole is perfect for a cold winter's night.

ingredients.name: haggis
ingredients.notes: traditional Scottish dish made from sheep's heart, liver, and lungs

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, whisk together the miso paste and red wine until smooth.
Add the cooked mushroom mixture, haggis, salt, and black pepper to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Spoon the mixture into 6 ramekins and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
